narrative.headline: 2022 Honda CR-V: steering leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 221 complaints with NHTSA. Steering is by far the most reported area at 50%, followed by forward collision avoidance (19%). No fires and no deaths are reported across any area. Forward collision avoidance stands out because, despite fewer reports than steering, it is tied to the most injuries (3) of any area, so the driver-assist features are worth testing carefully.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 110 reports (50%), half of all complaints, tied to 1 crash. No injuries or fires reported. Pay attention to how the car tracks and steers on a test drive.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 42 reports (19%), tied to 1 crash and 3 injuries, the most injuries of any area. Worth checking how the collision-avoidance features behave.

issues.blurb: 12 reports (5%), with 1 injury and no crashes or fires. The smallest of the top complaint areas; watch for unexpected warning lights.
